title: Reusable Layered Papercraft Poster

imagePrompts.prompt: Create a reusable layered papercraft poster with layered papercraft scene with visible cut-paper depth, stacked shadows, and tactile handmade texture. Create a premium vintage travel magazine cover poster for Lisbon in a vertical 4:5 composition. Automatically identify and feature the city's most iconic landmark as the dominant central focal point. The design should feel like a collectible luxury travel magazine cover, blending vintage explorer journals, editorial design, and sophisticated desti. Destination: Lisbon. Paper Layers: stacked paper-cut hills, tram tracks, tiled facades, river edge, and tiny street details. Landmark Set: yellow tram, azulejo wall, ocean overlook, and castle silhouette. Depth System: foreground street layer, midground city blocks, background sky and water layers. Lighting Style: warm late-afternoon paper shadows with visible cut edges. Title Text: LISBON. Subject: Make Lisbon the clear hero subject. Preserve the source prompt's subject hierarchy and avoid adding unrelated people or props. Scene and Context: Place the subject in Lisbon. Keep the background details useful to the concept rather than decorative filler. Composition and Layout: Use foreground street layer, midground city blocks, background sky and water layers. Keep the layout faithful to this template's own visual mechanic and do not introduce unrelated layout systems. Style and Rendering: Render in layered papercraft destination poster visual finish preserving these source cues: layered paper construction, destination landmark stack, visible paper edge depth. Keep this template's fixed material, texture, camera, illustration, or UI finish consistent across variants. Color and Lighting: Use the color system named or implied by the original prompt, anchored by: Create a premium vintage travel magazine cover poster for Lisbon in a vertical 4:5 composition. Lighting should match the template mood, separate the hero subject from the background, and stay readable at thumbnail size. Visible Typography: English-only, Latin alphabet only. Use one concise English headline or short English labels only when the template needs visible writing; otherwise keep visible writing minimal, intentional, and clean. Quality Restrictions: Avoid random letters, watermarks, malformed hands or faces, broken anatomy, duplicate logos, noisy background clutter, unreadable microcopy, and off-style artifacts.
